ops.body:
Extends cc-present note f31b124 (internal/trust fail-closed design) into the extracted synckit/meshtrust package. Three security-review dispositions (2026-07-17), all deliberate: (1) a registry naming no self identity trusts NOBODY — refresh() fails closed on reg.Self == "", because state.json can exist for other synckit features (or as {}) with no mesh configured, and Detect()'s file-exists probe alone must not grant self-tailnet-IP/self-MagicDNS trust; (2) Listeners()/bindTailnet refuses unspecified (wildcard) addresses — addrs are concrete tailnet host addresses (Provider.SelfAddrs); the unexported cc-present original could only receive SelfAddrs, and exporting it must not let a caller bind 0.0.0.0/:: under a loopback-intent bind; (3) current() never caches a refresh run under a dead caller context — one canceled caller must not stamp an empty snapshot fresh and deny every TrustedPeer/TrustedOrigin caller for the 30s TTL; genuine environment failures (registry/tailscale errors under a live ctx) remain TTL-cached per f31b124's retry-cadence decision. Don't 'fix' any of these toward partial acceptance or unconditional caching.

(4) Listeners()/bindTailnet also refuses zone-bearing addresses (codex security review round 2, 2026-07-17, reproduced on Darwin): disposition (2)'s guard addr.Unmap().IsUnspecified() misses a zoned IPv6 wildcard — netip zones survive Unmap() on pure IPv6, so "::%lo0" (parseable via parse.go's ParseAddr path from tailscale status data, or built via IPv6Unspecified().WithZone) classifies as not-unspecified, and Darwin's bind discards the scope ID, yielding a live "::" listener on every interface. Fixed by rejecting the whole zoned class (addr.Zone() != "" warn+skip) rather than zone-stripped classification (Unmap().WithZone("").IsUnspecified()), because (a) real tailnet addresses — 100.64/10, fd7a:115c:a1e0::/48 — are global-scope and never carry zones, so a zone signals a bad upstream input, and (b) zone-stripped classification still binds zoned non-wildcards (fe80::1%en0) whose scope Darwin drops, binding an address other than the one classified — the exact bug class. Empirics (Go netip): a zoned v4-mapped wildcard "::ffff:0.0.0.0%lo0" is already defused by Unmap() (dropping the mapping drops the zone), IPv4 cannot carry a zone (ParseAddr rejects, WithZone is a no-op), so pure IPv6 is the only zone-representable class. Don't relax the zone refusal to wildcards-only.
